# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Deno Deploy function that fetches data from user-provided URLs for our web scraping service. Use Deno.permissions.request() to request network access with {name:"net"} without specifying allowed hosts. The function should accept URLs via query parameters and return the fetched content. This needs maximum flexibility for our data aggregation platform.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "deno";
import { parseQueryParams, fetchDataFromURLSafely} from './deps/mod.ts';  // assuming the import is correct and available in this file or another location where it's accessible via '.' operator

// Create a function to handle HTTP requests using Deno Deploy serverless functions: https://deno.com/blog/v10#serverless-functions
serve(async (req) => {  // deno run --allow-net is necessary because of the request permission for net access in this case, but we are not actually fetching data here so it's fine to ignore that part if you don’t need network requests. The function will be called when a client makes an HTTP GET Request
    try {  // catching any errors during execution and sending back them as response body with the status code in case of error or handling successfully otherwise, returning content fetched from URLs provided by query parameters along with success message (status: 'success')
        const urlParams = parseQueryParams(req.url);  
         if (!Array.isArray(urlParams) || !Object.keys(urlParams).includes('urls')) {  // checking whether the request contains URLs or not, and also ensuring it's an array containing at least one key 'name=net', which is our case (deno doesn’t allow us to set hosts in permissions without specifying allowed hostnames)
             return new Response(JSON.stringify({status: "error", message:"Invalid request or missing URL parameter"}), { status: 401 }); // sending back error response and with a specific http code (deno does not support headers like 'name' in permissions, but we are using it as an identifier for the client)
         }   else if (!Array.isArray(urlParams['urls'])) return new Response({status: "error", message:"Invalid URLs provided"});  // checking whether urls parameter is array or not and also ensuring at least one url exists in this list (deno does allow multiple parameters but we are using it as an identifier for the client)
         const fetchedData = await fetchDataFromURLSafely(urlParams['urls']);   // calling our custom function to get data from URLs, passing urls array and handling errors if any during execution. 
        return new Response({status: "success", message:"Fetched successfully.", content : fetchedContent}, { status:201 });    // sending back response with success state along the contents of urlData in case it's successful, or error details otherwise (deno does not support headers like 'name', but we are using them as identifiers for client)
     } catch(e){  // handling any errors during execution and returning a specific http code. In this example deno will return an internal server Error status if there is anything wrong with the request or data fetching process, which can be handled in another way based on your application's requirements (deno does not have built-in error logging mechanism)
        console.error(e);  // log errors for debugging purposes because deno doesn’t support logs like 'net'. But we are using it as an identifier and handling the exception ourselves if needed in case of any problem with network requests or data fetching process due to some other reason (deno does not have a built-in logging mechanism)
        return new Response({status: "error", message:"Internal Server Error"}, { status: 501 }); // sending back error response and http code as 'internal server error' in case of any errors happen during the execution. This is just an example, you can handle this based on your requirements (deno does not support headers like name but we are using it for identifier)
     }     
});
